info.description: AuthProxy is an open-source, embeddable integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS). It manages the connection lifecycle to 3rd party systems, allowing applications to call those systems through an authenticating proxy.
info.title: AuthProxy API

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Initiate a new connection to an external service through a connector

get.description: Get a specific connection by its UUID
get.summary: Get connection

post.description: Abort an in-progress connection setup, cleaning up credentials and deleting the connection
Abandon a reconfigure attempt on a ready connection by clearing setup_step and setup_error. The connection remains ready and its previously stored configuration continues to apply.

get.description: Fetch dynamic options for a data source defined in the current setup step
get.summary: Get data source options

post.description: Disconnect an existing connection and revoke its credentials

put.description: Force a connection to a specific state (admin operation)

post.description: Proxy an HTTP request through an authenticated connection to the external service

Re-run the credential-collection portion of setup against an existing Ready connection. Used for user-driven credential rotation and as the recovery path when a connection is unhealthy. For api-key, returns a fresh credentials form (no prior values pre-filled); on submit the existing credential row is soft-deleted and a new one inserted in the same transaction. For OAuth2, restarts at preconnect:0 if defined, otherwise re-initiates the OAuth redirect. The connection's lifecycle state stays Ready throughout.
Restart the configure phase for a completed connection, allowing re-entry of post-auth settings

Retry a connection setup that ended in a terminal failure state. Applies to any setup-phase failure: an auth-phase failure such as an OAuth token-exchange error (auth_failed) or a probe failure during verify (verify_failed). Clears the recorded error and either returns to the first preconnect step (if the connector defines one, so the user can correct any input that led to the failure) or re-initiates the auth flow from scratch.

post.description: Create a new rate limit resource

Evaluate which rate-limit rules would apply to a synthesized request, and whether each would limit it. Counters are NOT incremented — the endpoint uses Limiter.Peek to inspect counter state without writing. Useful for validating selectors / buckets / algorithms without sending real traffic.

post.description: Initiate or establish a session with the server. If successful, returns actor info. If not authenticated, returns a redirect URL for authentication.
Explicitly terminate the current session. Used when navigating away from the SPA.

get.description: Get the status of a background task by its encrypted task info
get.summary: Get task status

current_step.description: Current step number in the setup phase.

json_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the form fields.

total_steps.description: Total number of steps in the setup phase.

ui_schema.description: UI Schema for JSON Forms rendering.

redirect_url.description: URL to redirect the user to.

routes.DataSourceOptionJson.description: Data source option for form select fields

connector_id.description: ID of the connector to initiate the connection for.

connector_version.description: Version of the connector to initiate connection for; if not specified defaults to the primary version.

into_namespace.description:
The namespace to create the connection in. Must be the namespace of connector or a child namespace of that
namespace. Defaults to the connector namespace if not specified.

return_to_url.description: The URL to return to after the connection is completed.

actor_id.description: This should include any configuration the SPA needs

routes.ProxyRequest.description: Request to proxy an HTTP request through a connection

body_raw.description: Raw body bytes (base64 encoded if binary)

labels.description: Optional labels to attach to this request's log entry (merged with connection labels; request labels override)

routes.ProxyResponse.description: Response from a proxied HTTP request

body_json.description: JSON response body (if content-type is application/json)

data.description: Data is the form data submitted by the user for the current step.

return_to_url.description:
ReturnToUrl is required when the next step after form submission is an auth redirect.
The client should provide this so the OAuth callback knows where to return.

step_id.description: StepId is the id of the step being submitted. Must match the current setup step's id.

health_state.description:
Operational health signal (healthy, unhealthy). Distinct from State: a Ready connection
whose credentials have stopped working flips to unhealthy without changing State.

setup_step_id.description:
Current setup step if connection setup is in progress. Either a user-authored step id from
the connector definition or an apxy:* pseudo-step (e.g. apxy:verify, apxy:auth_failed).

state.description: Connection state (pending, connected, disconnecting, disconnected, error)

routes.SwaggerCreateConnectorRequest.description: Request to create a new connector

routes.SwaggerCreateConnectorVersionRequ.description: Request to create a new draft connector version

task_id.description: Task ID for tracking the disconnect operation

routes.SwaggerDryRunRequest.description: Dry-run input: a proxy-shaped request + request type + the identity it runs under

annotations.description: Annotations assigned to the encryption key

labels.description: Labels assigned to the encryption key

routes.SwaggerForceConnectorVersionState.description: Request to force a connector version state

routes.SwaggerForceStateRequest.description: Request to force a connection state

state.description: Target state (pending, connected, disconnecting, disconnected, error)

routes.SwaggerListRequestEventsResponse.description: Paginated list of request events entries

total.description: Total count of matching records (if requested)

routes.SwaggerPutKeyValueRequest.description: Request to set a label or annotation value

annotations.description: Annotations assigned to the rate limit

labels.description: Labels assigned to the rate limit
Labels associated with the request (merged from connection and per-request labels)

routes.SwaggerUpdateConnectionRequest.description: Request to update a connection's labels

annotations.description: Annotations to set on the connection (replaces all existing annotations)

labels.description: Labels to set on the connection (replaces all existing labels)

routes.SwaggerUpdateConnectorRequest.description: Request to update a connector or connector version
